Test Description: Positive-edge interrupt enable on all GPIO pads (8..39) with system IRQ routing; verifies group status set/clear and raw per-pin clear sequence across iterations.
Test Steps / Procedure:
1) Entry: test_case() optionally enables IRQ 87/88 depending on GPIO0/GPIO1 macros.
2) Enable corresponding system interrupt by writing MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO{0/1}_INTR based on macro.
3) For i=0..31: program per-pin posedge enable by write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4, 0x00020000).
4) wait_on(10); configure IO control groups 1..4 to inputs via writes 0x000000FF each; wait_on(10).
5) Enable group interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF).
6) For i=0..31: drive external reg 0xA0243ffc low; wait_on(10); set int_pend=1; drive 0xA0243ffc high to create rising edge; poll with timeout 2000: while(int_pend && --timeout>0) wait_on(10); on timeout: log error, test_err++ and break; then drive low and wait_on(10).
7) Exit: finish(test_err).
8) Default_IRQHandler(): compute wr_val=(1<<i); set int_pend=0; optionally log; rdata_grp=read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); mask group by writing EN1=0; if rdata_grp==0 -> error++ else success; for j=0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + j*4, 0x00010000) to clear raw; wait_on(2); read back group status; if !=0 -> error++; clear sysreg RAW_STCR1 per GPIO0/GPIO1 and verify readback bit cleared; re-enable group EN1=0xFFFFFFFF; clear GIC IRQ 87/88 accordingly.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- For each i in [0..31], ISR observed (no timeout) and int_pend cleared by ISR.
- In ISR: group status non-zero on entry; after per-pin raw clear loop, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 reads 0.
- System RAW_STCR1 cleared for the selected GPIO instance; subsequent readback shows bit deasserted.
- Overall: finish(test_err) with 0 errors.
Gap Analysis: Relies on external pad-driving via 0xA0243ffc; no per-pin isolation of edges (group enable remains global); no debounce/noise filtering; DMA/polling paths untested; behavior depends on GPIO0/GPIO1 macro selection at build time.
